scenarios.description: clone() on empty Document returns a distinct instance with identical base state
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = new Document("http://example.com"); // no children, default outputSettings and parser
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document != doc; returned.baseUri() equals "http://example.com"; returned.childNodeSize() == 0; returned.outputSettings().equals(doc.outputSettings()); returned.parser().equals(doc.parser())
pseudocode: assert copy != doc; assert copy.baseUri().equals("http://example.com"); assert copy.childNodeSize() == 0; assert copy.outputSettings().equals(doc.outputSettings()); assert copy.parser().equals(doc.parser());

scenarios.description: clone() deep copies Document with one child node; modifying clone child does not affect original
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = Document.createShell("http://base"); doc.body().appendElement("p").text("para"); // one child in body
scenarios.expectedResult: copy.childNodeSize() == doc.childNodeSize(); modifying copy.body().selectFirst("p").text("new") does not change doc.body().selectFirst("p").text() which remains "para"
pseudocode: TEST TC02 clone with one child

scenarios.description: clone() preserves multiple child nodes; each child cloned separately
scenarios.expectedResult: copy.childNodeSize() == doc.childNodeSize(); for each index i, copy.childNodes().get(i) != doc.childNodes().get(i) and has same tag name
pseudocode: TEST TC03 clone with multiple children
assert copy.childNodeSize() == doc.childNodeSize(); assert copy.childNodes().get(1) != doc.childNodes().get(1); assert copy.childNodes().get(1).nodeName().equals(doc.childNodes().get(1).nodeName());

scenarios.description: clone() returns deep copy such that modifying outputSettings of clone does not affect original
scenarios.precondition: Document doc = new Document("ns","base"); // default outputSettings
scenarios.expectedResult: copy.outputSettings().charset() initially equals doc.outputSettings().charset(); after copy.charset(Charset.forName("US-ASCII")), doc.charset() remains unchanged UTF-8; copy.charset() is US-ASCII
pseudocode: TEST TC04 clone and modify outputSettings
